---
title: "Chapter 6 - Atma-Samyama Yoga"
---

<div class="chapter-title-sanskrit">आत्मसंयमयोगः</div>

<nav class="chapter-nav"><a href="chapter-05.html">Previous chapter</a><a href="chapter-07.html">Next chapter</a></nav>

<div class="verse-list">
<article class="verse" id="v-6-1" data-reference="6.1">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-1">6.1</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He who performs the action that should be done, without relying on the fruit of action, is both a renunciant and a <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yogi</span>, not one without fire and not one without action.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अनाश्रितः कर्मफलं कार्यं कर्म करोति यः ।<br>
स संन्यासी च योगी च न निरग्निर्न चाक्रियः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-2" data-reference="6.2">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-2">6.2</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Understand that what they call renunciation is <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." aria-label="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." title="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." data-glossary-id="pandava">Pāṇḍava</span>; for no one becomes a <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yogī</span> without renouncing intention.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यं संन्यासमिति प्राहुर्योगं तं विद्धि पाण्डव ।<br>
न ह्यसंन्यस्तसङ्कल्पो योगी भवति कश्चन ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-3" data-reference="6.3">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-3">6.3</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For a sage who seeks to ascend to <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, action is said to be the means;<br>
for the same one, when established in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, stillness is said to be the means.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">आरुरुक्षोर्मुनेर्योगं कर्म कारणमुच्यते ।<br>
योगारूढस्य तस्यैव शमः कारणमुच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-4" data-reference="6.4">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-4">6.4</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For when one is not attached to sense-objects or to actions, and has renounced all intentions, then one is said to have ascended to <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यदा हि नेन्द्रियार्थेषु न कर्मस्वनुषज्जते ।<br>
सर्वसङ्कल्पसंन्यासी योगारूढस्तदोच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-5" data-reference="6.5">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-5">6.5</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">One should lift the self by the self; one should not bring the self down. For the self is indeed the self’s friend, and the self is also the self’s enemy.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">उद्धरेदात्मनात्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत् ।<br>
आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मनः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-6" data-reference="6.6">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-6">6.6</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For that person, the self is the friend of the self by whom the self has been conquered by the self; but for one whose self is not conquered, the self alone acts as an enemy, like an enemy.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">बन्धुरात्मात्मनस्तस्य येनात्मैवात्मना जितः ।<br>
अनात्मनस्तु शत्रुत्वे वर्तेतात्मैव शत्रुवत् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-7" data-reference="6.7">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-7">6.7</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For one who has conquered the self and is tranquil, the Supreme Self is well settled, in cold and heat, and in pleasure and pain, as well as in honor and dishonor.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">जितात्मनः प्रशान्तस्य परमात्मा समाहितः ।<br>
शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु तथा मानापमानयोः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-8" data-reference="6.8">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-8">6.8</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yogi</span> whose self is satisfied with knowledge and realization, who is steadfast and has conquered the senses, and to whom a lump of earth, a stone, and gold are the same, is called integrated.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ज्ञानविज्ञानतृप्तात्मा कूटस्थो विजितेन्द्रियः ।<br>
युक्त इत्युच्यते योगी समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चनः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-9" data-reference="6.9">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-9">6.9</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He is distinguished by equal-mindedness toward benefactors, friends, enemies, the indifferent, mediators, the hateful, relatives, the righteous, and the sinful.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सुहृन्मित्रार्युदासीनमध्यस्थद्वेष्यबन्धुषु ।<br>
साधुष्वपि च पापेषु समबुद्धिर्विशिष्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-10" data-reference="6.10">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-10">6.10</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The yogin should constantly discipline himself, dwelling in solitude, alone, with mind and self controlled, free from desire and free from possessions.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">योगी युञ्जीत सततमात्मानं रहसि स्थितः ।<br>
एकाकी यतचित्तात्मा निराशीरपरिग्रहः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-11" data-reference="6.11">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-11">6.11</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Having set up for oneself a firm seat in a clean place, neither too high nor too low, made of cloth, deerskin, and kusa grass.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">शुचौ देशे प्रतिष्ठाप्य स्थिरमासनमात्मनः ।<br>
नात्युच्छ्रितं नातिनीचं चैलाजिनकुशोत्तरम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-12" data-reference="6.12">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-12">6.12</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">There, having made the mind one-pointed and having restrained the activities of mind and senses, one should sit on the seat and practice <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> for the purification of the self.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तत्रैकाग्रं मनः कृत्वा यतचित्तेन्द्रियक्रियः ।<br>
उपविश्यासने युञ्ज्याद्योगमात्मविशुद्धये ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-13" data-reference="6.13">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-13">6.13</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Holding the body, head, and neck straight and still,<br>
fixed, steady, gazing at the tip of his own nose, not looking around.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">समं कायशिरोग्रीवं धारयन्नचलं स्थिरः ।<br>
सम्प्रेक्ष्य नासिकाग्रं स्वं दिशश्चानवलोकयन् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-14" data-reference="6.14">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-14">6.14</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">With mind शांत and free from fear, established in the vow of celibacy, controlling the mind, with consciousness fixed on Me, let him sit, devoted to Me as the supreme.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">प्रशान्तात्मा विगतभीर्ब्रह्मचारिव्रते स्थितः ।<br>
मनः संयम्य मच्चित्तो युक्त आसीत मत्परः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-15" data-reference="6.15">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-15">6.15</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Thus, constantly disciplining the self in this way, the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yogī</span> with a controlled mind attains peace, the supreme <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." aria-label="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." title="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." data-glossary-id="nirvana">nirvāṇa</span>, abiding in Me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">युञ्जन्नेवं सदात्मानं योगी नियतमानसः ।<br>
शान्तिं निर्वाणपरमां मत्संस्थामधिगच्छति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-16" data-reference="6.16">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-16">6.16</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en"><span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">Yoga</span> is not for one who eats too much, nor for one who does not eat at all; nor for one who sleeps too much, nor for one who is always awake, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">नात्यश्नतस्तु योगोऽस्ति न चैकान्तमनश्नतः ।<br>
न चातिस्वप्नशीलस्य जाग्रतो नैव चार्जुन ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-17" data-reference="6.17">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-17">6.17</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For one whose eating and recreation are regulated, whose actions are regulated, and whose sleep and waking are regulated, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> becomes the destroyer of sorrow.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">युक्ताहारविहारस्य युक्तचेष्टस्य कर्मसु ।<br>
युक्तस्वप्नावबोधस्य योगो भवति दुःखहा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-18" data-reference="6.18">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-18">6.18</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">When the disciplined mind remains fixed solely in the Self, then he is said to be free from craving and established in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यदा विनियतं चित्तमात्मन्येवावतिष्ठते ।<br>
निःस्पृहः सर्वकामेभ्यो युक्त इत्युच्यते तदा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-19" data-reference="6.19">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-19">6.19</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">As a lamp in a windless place does not flicker, so is the yogin of controlled mind, practicing <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> of the Self.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यथा दीपो निवातस्थो नेङ्गते सोपमा स्मृता ।<br>
योगिनो यतचित्तस्य युञ्जतो योगमात्मनः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-20" data-reference="6.20">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-20">6.20</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">When the mind, restrained by the practice of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, becomes still, and when, seeing the self by the self, one is content in the self.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यत्रोपरमते चित्तं निरुद्धं योगसेवया ।<br>
यत्र चैवात्मनात्मानं पश्यन्नात्मनि तुष्यति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-21" data-reference="6.21">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-21">6.21</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">That supreme happiness, which is grasped by the intellect and beyond the senses, which one knows there, and from which one does not depart in truth.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सुखमात्यन्तिकं यत्तद् बुद्धिग्राह्यमतीन्द्रियम् ।<br>
वेत्ति यत्र न चैवायं स्थितश्चलति तत्त्वतः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-22" data-reference="6.22">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-22">6.22</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Having obtained that, he considers no other gain greater than it; established in which, he is not shaken even by the heaviest sorrow.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यं लब्ध्वा चापरं लाभं मन्यते नाधिकं ततः ।<br>
यस्मिन्स्थितो न दुःखेन गुरुणापि विचाल्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-23" data-reference="6.23">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-23">6.23</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Know that <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> to be the severance of union with suffering; that <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> should be practiced with resolute determination and a mind that does not lose heart.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तं विद्याद् दुःखसंयोगवियोगं योगसंज्ञितम् ।<br>
स निश्चयेन योक्तव्यो योगोऽनिर्विण्णचेतसा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-24" data-reference="6.24">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-24">6.24</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Abandoning completely all desires born of resolve, and restraining the whole group of senses on every side by the mind alone.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सङ्कल्पप्रभवान्कामांस्त्यक्त्वा सर्वानशेषतः ।<br>
मनसैवेन्द्रियग्रामं विनियम्य समन्ततः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-25" data-reference="6.25">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-25">6.25</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Little by little, with the intellect held firm by steadiness, one should bring the mind to rest in the Self and think of nothing at all.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">शनैः शनैरुपरमेद् बुद्ध्या धृतिगृहीतया ।<br>
आत्मसंस्थं मनः कृत्वा न किञ्चिदपि चिन्तयेत् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-26" data-reference="6.26">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-26">6.26</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Whenever the restless, unsteady mind wanders away, one should restrain it from that and bring it back under the control of the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Atman: Self or inner principle; kept distinct from ordinary ego or personality." aria-label="Atman: Self or inner principle; kept distinct from ordinary ego or personality." title="Atman: Self or inner principle; kept distinct from ordinary ego or personality." data-glossary-id="atman">Atman</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यतो यतो निश्चरति मनश्चञ्चलमस्थिरम् ।<br>
ततस्ततो नियम्यैतदात्मन्येव वशं नयेत् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-27" data-reference="6.27">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-27">6.27</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For the yogin whose mind is peaceful, whose <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajas</span> has been quieted, who is free from sin and become <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>, supreme happiness comes to him.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">प्रशान्तमनसं ह्येनं योगिनं सुखमुत्तमम् ।<br>
उपैति शान्तरजसं ब्रह्मभूतमकल्मषम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-28" data-reference="6.28">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-28">6.28</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Thus, constantly disciplining the self, the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yogi</span>, freed from impurity, easily attains supreme bliss through contact with <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">युञ्जन्नेवं सदात्मानं योगी विगतकल्मषः ।<br>
सुखेन ब्रह्मसंस्पर्शमत्यन्तं सुखमश्नुते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-29" data-reference="6.29">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-29">6.29</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The one whose self is disciplined by <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> sees the Self abiding in all beings, and all beings in the Self, and sees equally everywhere.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सर्वभूतस्थमात्मानं सर्वभूतानि चात्मनि ।<br>
ईक्षते योगयुक्तात्मा सर्वत्र समदर्शनः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-30" data-reference="6.30">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-30">6.30</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">One who sees Me everywhere and sees everything in Me, for him I am not lost, and he is not lost to Me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यो मां पश्यति सर्वत्र सर्वं च मयि पश्यति ।<br>
तस्याहं न प्रणश्यामि स च मे न प्रणश्यति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-31" data-reference="6.31">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-31">6.31</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yogī</span> who is established in unity worships Me, dwelling in all beings. In all conditions, he abides in Me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सर्वभूतस्थितं यो मां भजत्येकत्वमास्थितः ।<br>
सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि स योगी मयि वर्तते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-32" data-reference="6.32">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-32">6.32</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en"><span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>, the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yogi</span> who sees everything as equal everywhere, by comparing it with his own self—whether happiness or sorrow—is regarded as the highest.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">आत्मौपम्येन सर्वत्र समं पश्यति योऽर्जुन ।<br>
सुखं वा यदि वा दुःखं स योगी परमो मतः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-33" data-reference="6.33">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-33">6.33</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Madhusudana: Epithet of Krishna: slayer of Madhu." aria-label="Madhusudana: Epithet of Krishna: slayer of Madhu." title="Madhusudana: Epithet of Krishna: slayer of Madhu." data-glossary-id="madhusudana">Madhusūdana</span>, this <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> that you have spoken of as evenness, I do not see its enduring steadiness, because of the restlessness of the mind.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">योऽयं योगस्त्वया प्रोक्तः साम्येन मधुसूदन ।<br>
एतस्याहं न पश्यामि चञ्चलत्वात्स्थितिं स्थिराम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-34" data-reference="6.34">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-34">6.34</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For the mind is restless, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." aria-label="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." title="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." data-glossary-id="krishna">Kṛṣṇa</span>, turbulent, powerful, and stubborn; I regard its restraint as difficult as restraining the wind.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">चञ्चलं हि मनः कृष्ण प्रमाथि बलवद् दृढम् ।<br>
तस्याहं निग्रहं मन्ये वायोरिव सुदुष्करम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-35" data-reference="6.35">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-35">6.35</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Undoubtedly, O mighty-armed one, the mind is difficult to restrain and restless. But, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." aria-label="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." title="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." data-glossary-id="kaunteya">Kaunteya</span>, it is brought under control by practice and by detachment.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">असंशयं महाबाहो मनो दुर्निग्रहं चलम् ।<br>
अभ्यासेन तु कौन्तेय वैराग्येण च गृह्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-36" data-reference="6.36">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-36">6.36</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en"><span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">Yoga</span> is difficult to attain for one whose self is not controlled; but for one who has self-control and strives, it can be attained by proper means, in my view.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">असंयतात्मना योगो दुष्प्राप इति मे मतिः ।<br>
वश्यात्मना तु यतता शक्योऽवाप्तुमुपायतः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-37" data-reference="6.37">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-37">6.37</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en"><span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span> said: If one is not self-controlled but is endowed with faith, and whose mind has strayed from <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." aria-label="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." title="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." data-glossary-id="krishna">Krishna</span>, what destination does he attain, having failed to reach perfection in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अयतिः श्रद्धयोपेतो योगाच्चलितमानसः ।<br>
अप्राप्य योगसंसिद्धिं कां गतिं कृष्ण गच्छति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-38" data-reference="6.38">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-38">6.38</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Does such a one, fallen from both, not perish like a shattered cloud, deprived of support, O mighty-armed one, deluded on the path of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कच्चिन्नोभयविभ्रष्टश्छिन्नाभ्रमिव नश्यति ।<br>
अप्रतिष्ठो महाबाहो विमूढो ब्रह्मणः पथि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-39" data-reference="6.39">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-39">6.39</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." aria-label="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." title="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." data-glossary-id="krishna">Kṛṣṇa</span>, you are indeed fit to dispel this doubt of mine completely. No one else besides you is capable of removing this doubt.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">एतन्मे संशयं कृष्ण छेत्तुमर्हस्यशेषतः ।<br>
त्वदन्यः संशयस्यास्य छेत्ता न ह्युपपद्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-40" data-reference="6.40">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-40">6.40</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en"><span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, there is no destruction for him, neither here nor hereafter. For no one who does good, my dear, ever comes to a bad end.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">पार्थ नैवेह नामुत्र विनाशस्तस्य विद्यते ।<br>
न हि कल्याणकृत्कश्चिद् दुर्गतिं तात गच्छति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-41" data-reference="6.41">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-41">6.41</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Having attained the worlds of the righteous and dwelling there for many long years, the one who has fallen from <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> is born in the house of the pure and the prosperous.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">प्राप्य पुण्यकृतां लोकानुषित्वा शाश्वतीः समाः ।<br>
शुचीनां श्रीमतां गेहे योगभ्रष्टोऽभिजायते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-42" data-reference="6.42">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-42">6.42</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Or else he is born in the family of wise yogis; for such a birth is very difficult to obtain in this world.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अथवा योगिनामेव कुले भवति धीमताम् ।<br>
एतद्धि दुर्लभतरं लोके जन्म यदीदृशम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-43" data-reference="6.43">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-43">6.43</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">There he regains that union of understanding from a former body, and then, O Kurunandana, he strives onward again toward perfection.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तत्र तं बुद्धिसंयोगं लभते पौर्वदेहिकम् ।<br>
यतते च ततो भूयः संसिद्धौ कुरुनन्दन ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-44" data-reference="6.44">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-44">6.44</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">By that very prior practice, he is drawn on, even against his will; even the seeker of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> goes beyond the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span> of sound.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">पूर्वाभ्यासेन तेनैव ह्रियते ह्यवशोऽपि सः ।<br>
जिज्ञासुरपि योगस्य शब्दब्रह्मातिवर्तते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-45" data-reference="6.45">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-45">6.45</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yogi</span>, striving diligently and purified of all blemishes, perfected through many births, then attains the supreme goal.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">प्रयत्नाद्यतमानस्तु योगी संशुद्धकिल्बिषः ।<br>
अनेकजन्मसंसिद्धस्ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-46" data-reference="6.46">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-46">6.46</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yogi</span> is greater than ascetics, and is thought to be greater than the jnanis as well. The <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yogi</span> is greater than those devoted to action; therefore, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>, become a <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yogi</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तपस्विभ्योऽधिको योगी ज्ञानिभ्योऽपि मतोऽधिकः ।<br>
कर्मिभ्यश्चाधिको योगी तस्माद्योगी भवार्जुन ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-6-47" data-reference="6.47">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-6-47">6.47</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Among all yogis too, the one who worships Me with faith, with his inner self devoted to Me, is regarded by Me as the most closely united.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">योगिनामपि सर्वेषां मद्गतेनान्तरात्मना ।<br>
श्रद्धावान्भजते यो मां स मे युक्ततमो मतः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

</div>
